title: Market Beat — News Impact Analysis
subtitle: 14 headlines · 3 levels · 42 tickers · Gulf war risk meets ADP & Beige Book; AVGO/CRWD print tonight

macro.headline: Jobs Week + Beige Book today; payrolls Friday is the hike-odds swing event under new Chair Warsh
macro.regime: HAWKISH TILT — Gulf-driven oil shock has lifted year-end hike odds to ~42% (CME FedWatch); virtually zero 2026 cut probability priced.

keyEvents.label: Fed (FOMC)
keyEvents.detail: Warsh's 1st meeting as Chair; ~3.5% chance of a June hike, but markets watch for removal of dovish forward-guidance language.

keyEvents.label: Jobs (NFP)
keyEvents.detail: May payrolls fcst ~95k (vs +115k April); U-rate 4.3%. The week's main event for hike-odds repricing.

keyEvents.label: CPI
keyEvents.detail: May inflation; last print +3.8% YoY, hottest since 2024 on Gulf-driven gasoline pass-through.

keyEvents.label: ADP Jobs
keyEvents.detail: May private payrolls fcst +110k (vs +109k April). NFP appetizer — strong print kills the cut narrative outright.

keyEvents.label: Beige Book
keyEvents.detail: First Beige Book under Chair Warsh; districts' read on Gulf-shock pass-through to prices is the key signal.

week.event: ISM Manufacturing 54.0 (5-yr high) · Alphabet $80B AI capital raise
JOLTS 7.62M (2-yr high); HPE +19.5% on AI servers; PANW & MRVL halo
ADP, ISM Services, Beige Book; AVGO + CRWD + AVGO earnings after bell
Claims, Productivity, Challenger Layoffs; Ciena earnings
NONFARM PAYROLLS (May) + U-rate + AHE — hike-odds swing event

news.headline: Gulf escalates: Iran fires missiles at Kuwait & Bahrain; U.S. strikes Qeshm; WTI rips +2% to $95

L1.text: U.S. Central Command says Iran fired ballistic missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain overnight; both salvos failed or were thwarted, and U.S. forces hit back at Iran's Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz. Brent settled +1.1% at $97.05 while WTI ripped ~2% to $95.40 — both up for a third straight session as US-Iran peace talks stalled. Tanker transits through Hormuz remain well below pre-conflict levels.

L2.text: Beneficiaries cascade through the integrated oil chain (XOM, CVX, COP, OXY, EOG), refiners (VLO, PSX, MPC), oil-services peers (SLB, HAL, BKR), and LNG names (LNG, CHK) as Gulf supply risk re-prices. Defense primes LMT/RTX/NOC/GD/LDOS catch a sympathy bid on missile-defense and air-frame demand. Airlines (DAL, UAL, AAL, LUV) and cruise operators (CCL, RCL) bear the jet-fuel cost shock as suppliers raise hedged surcharges.

L3.text: Crude above $95 for a third session pushes 5y5y inflation breakevens higher and re-prices Fed hike odds — CME FedWatch now shows ~42% probability of a year-end hike, the highest since Warsh took the chair. A sustained Hormuz disruption ($100+ Brent path Goldman flagged) raises stagflation risk: every $10 sustained on Brent adds ~0.4pp to headline CPI and shaves ~0.2pp off G7 GDP. Defensive sectors (staples, utilities, healthcare) outperform; long-duration growth (software/internet) under pressure as discount rates rise.

beneficiaries: XOM — integrated mega-cap leverage to Brent >$95
VLO/PSX — refining cracks widen on Gulf supply fear
LMT/RTX — missile-defense reload as Patriots/THAAD intercepts mount
victims: DAL/UAL/AAL — jet-fuel cost shock hits Q2/Q3 unit costs
LUV — un-hedged exposure historically worst in sector
CCL/RCL — bunker fuel + consumer-sentiment double-hit
AMZN/WMT logistics — diesel pass-through pressure
TLT — long-duration Treasuries sold on hike repricing

timeline.D: Energy ETFs (XLE, OIH) lead tape; defense names gap up at open; airlines/cruise sold.
timeline.W: EIA inventories Thu + NFP Fri test whether oil-shock is sticky or fades on de-escalation headlines.
timeline.M: If Brent holds >$95 into June 17 FOMC, Warsh's first decision becomes a live hike debate.
timeline.L: Persistent Hormuz friction structurally re-rates oil-services capex and LNG export expansion (LNG, TELL, NEXT).

news.headline: SpaceX sets record $75B IPO at $135/share — $1.75T valuation, roadshow Thursday

L1.text: Elon Musk's SpaceX plans to fix its IPO price at $135 per share to raise a record-shattering $75B, sources told Reuters — selling 555.6M shares for a $1.75T valuation when the Nasdaq roadshow opens Thursday. The move skips the traditional price-range stage entirely, signaling extraordinary book-build confidence. Prospectus discloses $697M of Tesla Megapack purchases and $131M of Cybertrucks across 2024-25 — and >75% of Q1 capex ($10.1B) was AI-related.

L2.text: Underwriting bonanza for the bulge-bracket peers (GS, MS, JPM, BAC) — fees on a $75B deal alone could clear $1.5-2B. Smaller listed space peers RKLB, ASTS, IRDM, PL face direct multiple-compression risk as a public SpaceX becomes the comparable; legacy aerospace (LMT, BA, NOC) loses Pentagon launch-share narrative. Tesla benefits via Musk halo + disclosed commercial ties — combined-entity rumors (TSLA + SpaceX) resurface on the prospectus.

L3.text: Largest IPO in history reopens the mega-deal window after a 4-year drought — bullish read-through for CART, KLAR, STRP, and the broader AI-private pipeline (OpenAI, Stripe, Databricks). Macro absorbing $75B of equity supply in one week is a real liquidity event — short-end Treasury bills and money-market AUM should see outflows, and IPO ETF (IPO, IPOX) re-rates. Also a confidence vote for the Trump-era deregulatory IPO regime under Atkins SEC.

beneficiaries: GS/MS — lead underwriter fee pool on largest IPO ever
TSLA — Musk halo + disclosed SpaceX commercial linkages
IPO ETF — opens the mega-deal window
victims: RKLB — public-comp multiple compresses against SpaceX
ASTS — direct-to-cell competition with Starlink reignites
LMT/BA — Pentagon launch market-share narrative loses
IRDM — Starlink consumer pull-through pressure

timeline.D: Watch RKLB/ASTS/IRDM action; underwriter bank stocks bid as fee pool firms up.
timeline.W: Roadshow opens Thu; order-book whisper numbers drive sentiment Mon-Wed next week.
timeline.M: Pricing likely 2-3 weeks out; aftermarket open prints the new mega-cap comp.
timeline.L: If priced cleanly, IPO window stays open through Q4 — biggest backlog of private unicorns ever sees a path.

news.headline: Broadcom (AVGO) earnings after the bell — record-high stock into $52B AI-revenue test

L1.text: Broadcom (AVGO $481.57, +4.7% Tue) prints FQ2 after the close — Street looks for $14.9B revenue (~+18% YoY) and AI-silicon revenue tracking toward a $52B annualized run-rate by FY26 vs the $60-90B FY27 SAM CEO Hock Tan flagged. Stock is up 51% over 3 months and 93% over 12 months but has lagged the SMH (+75% YTD vs AVGO +39%). The whisper is for a custom-ASIC backlog update and clarity on the 4th hyperscaler customer.

L2.text: Halo or unwind across the custom-silicon and optical complex: MRVL (just +32.5% Mon on Huang endorsement), CRDO, ANET, COHR, LITE all key off ASIC and 1.6T optics commentary. Peer NVDA benefits if AVGO confirms hyperscaler XPU demand is incremental (not cannibalizing GPUs); AMD bears the brunt if ASIC share takes another leg up. Suppliers TSM, MU, ASML get pulled by capex-intensity reads.

L3.text: Test of the 'positioning is dangerously one-sided' thesis — BofA Fund Manager Survey shows 73% of allocators are long semis, the most crowded trade on the Street. A clean beat-and-raise extends the rally; any guide-down on China export licenses or hyperscaler backlog visibility cracks the AI cap-ex narrative just as $700B+ of 2026 hyperscaler capex is being committed. Rate-sensitivity is high — long-duration growth re-rates on every basis-point move with Brent at $95.

beneficiaries: AVGO — clean print extends the ASIC arms-dealer thesis
MRVL — confirms custom-silicon TAM expansion narrative
NVDA — incremental, not substitutional, demand read
victims: AMD — merchant GPU loses more share-of-wallet to ASIC
INTC — outside the custom-silicon halo
SOXS (bearish ETF) — print typically punishes shorts

timeline.D: After-hours move sets the AM tape for the entire SMH; PT hike cycle starts Thu.
timeline.W: Read-through to ORCL (Tue) and Adobe (Thu next wk); options vol around $52B AI run-rate target.
timeline.M: ASIC backlog + 4th hyperscaler disclosure tested by hyperscaler capex prints.
timeline.L: Confirms or breaks the 'AI silicon eats merchant GPU share at the margin' structural call.

news.headline: CrowdStrike (CRWD) earnings tonight — record-high stock, guidance bar is the trap

L1.text: CrowdStrike reports FQ1 after the bell — Street consensus: revenue +23.5% YoY, EPS +46.6%. Stock hit a fresh ATH Monday after nearly doubling in 3 months on AI-security momentum (Falcon Flex, Charlotte AI). Recent Moody's upgrade to Baa2 and PT raises from Jefferies/Benchmark/Cantor set a high bar — the trap is FY guide and ARR commentary, not the print itself. Last quarter Q2 guide spooked the tape; positioning is heavy.

L2.text: Read-through to peers PANW (already beat Tue, +61% YTD), ZS, FTNT, S, OKTA, NET — a soft guide hits the entire cybersecurity bid that has been the cleanest software trade of 2026. Suppliers AVGO/SMCI (server pull) and customers in the bank/insurance MFA stack (V, MA, PYPL) all read net-neutral. SentinelOne (S) is the asymmetric loser as the closest pure-play comp.

L3.text: Cyber is now ~12% of enterprise software spend (Gartner) and the only enterprise category with 20%+ growth at scale — a CRWD miss would trigger a crowding-unwind across the long-software book just as macro adds rate-hike pressure. Conversely, a beat-and-raise validates the 'AI-security is a separate budget line' narrative that protects opex from the broader IT-budget squeeze hitting CRM/WDAY/NOW share-of-wallet.

beneficiaries: PANW — peer re-rate on AI-security validation
ZS — secure-edge halo if Falcon Flex commentary positive
victims: S (SentinelOne) — closest pure-play comp, asymmetric downside
OKTA — identity narrative loses share if Falcon adds IdP modules
CRM/WDAY — broader software unwind if guide soft

timeline.D: AH move sets cyber-ETF (HACK, CIBR) direction; positioning is heavy long.
timeline.W: Read into AVGO + Ciena Thu; PT recalibration cycle through Fri.
timeline.M: Falcon Flex deal-count vs ARR is the structural metric to watch.
timeline.L: Test of whether AI-security stays a separate budget line through a slowing IT-spend cycle.

news.headline: Alphabet plans $80B AI capital raise; $10B equity placement to Berkshire Hathaway

L1.text: Alphabet confirmed Monday it will raise ~$80B in equity — $30B public offering + $40B at-the-market program + $10B private placement to Berkshire Hathaway — to fund AI compute capacity that is 'exceeding available supply'. This sits on top of the $180-190B 2026 capex guide announced at Google I/O. GOOGL slipped in after-hours but the stock has more than doubled in 12 months, outperforming all megacap peers.

L2.text: Direct AI-supply-chain windfall: NVDA (GPU), AVGO (TPU custom silicon), MRVL (interconnect), TSM (foundry), MU/HBM. Data-center physical layer is the second derivative — VRT (Vertiv), ETN, HUBB, PWR, CSCO, ANET, and cooling/power infrastructure (CEG, VST, TLN, GEV) all benefit as the $700B 2026 hyperscaler capex bucket gets re-confirmed. Berkshire taking equity on Buffett's behalf endorses the AI thesis at the very top.

L3.text: $80B of fresh equity supply is real, but small vs the $1B+ daily NVDA flow — bigger signal is that hyperscalers are now diluting equity rather than just issuing debt to fund AI, which compresses near-term FCF/share and raises the bar for AI revenue conversion. Power-grid bottleneck moves to center stage — Pichai's own comment was 'compute, land, power, supply chain' — bullish for utilities with co-location data-center contracts (CEG, VST, TLN) and grid-modernization (GEV, ETN, HUBB).

beneficiaries: BRK.B — Berkshire gets institutional AI exposure at sponsored price
victims: GOOGL — equity dilution + FCF compression near-term
GOOG — same
META/MSFT/AMZN — implicit pressure to commit to bigger capex

timeline.D: GOOGL tape choppy on dilution; AI-pick-and-shovel basket bid.
timeline.W: Berkshire 13F watch for confirmation; ATM offering pacing reports.
timeline.M: Other hyperscalers' next capex guides re-rate higher in sympathy.
timeline.L: Marks the regime shift from debt-funded to equity-funded AI capex cycle.

news.headline: FERC waiver clears path to restart Three Mile Island for Microsoft AI data centers

L1.text: FERC granted Constellation Energy (CEG) a waiver to speed up the grid connection of the restarted Three Mile Island (Crane Clean Energy Center) — the 835MW reactor will sell power directly to Microsoft data centers under a 20-year PPA. Constellation had previously warned PJM congestion could delay startup to 2031; the waiver pulls that materially forward. Nuclear and uranium names rallied — UEC +13%, OKLO +10%, SMR +8%, CCJ +7% Tue.

L2.text: Direct beneficiaries: Constellation (operator), competing nuclear restarts at Palisades (Holtec)/Duane Arnold (NEE) get implicit regulatory tailwind. Uranium fuel-cycle peers CCJ, UEC, DNN, NXE, URA ETF re-rate on demand-pull. SMR specialists OKLO, SMR, BWXT, BW catch sympathy bid. Hyperscaler customers (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META) all benefit from the precedent — power-sourcing for data centers is the single biggest gating constraint to 2027 AI buildouts.

L3.text: Confirms the structural 'nuclear is the only baseload that scales with AI' thesis — DOE forecasts data-center load will hit ~10% of US electricity by 2030 vs ~4% today, and only firmable nuclear (or natural gas) can meet 24/7 zero-carbon PPAs. Macro: regulated utility re-rating cycle continues (XLU outperforming year-to-date), and the political consensus around AI infrastructure has rare bipartisan momentum under the Trump-Wright DOE.

beneficiaries: CEG — direct operator + PJM friction reduced
VST/TLN — peer comp re-rate on hyperscaler PPA template
MSFT — power-sourcing risk reduced for OpenAI compute
victims: PJM gas peakers — partial baseload displacement
Renewables-only IPPs without firm contracts — relative loser

timeline.D: Uranium ETF (URA) leads; small-cap nuclear (UEC, UUUU) momentum chase.
timeline.W: Read-through to Holtec/Palisades licensing milestones; NEE Duane Arnold update.
timeline.M: Tests whether PJM capacity-auction price clears reflect data-center demand.
timeline.L: Cements nuclear-baseload PPA as the standard hyperscaler power template through 2030.

news.headline: GameStop +9% after-hours: record Q1 EPS, revenue beat, new $2B buyback through 2029

L1.text: GameStop reported Q1 adj. EPS $0.30 vs $0.16 consensus (nearly 2x), revenue $835.3M vs $766.6M est (vs $732.4M YoY) — record quarterly net income of $389.6M and record Q1 operating income of $143.3M. SG&A fell to $201.6M from $228.1M. Cash + securities + digital assets hit $9.7B. Board authorized a new $2B share buyback through 2029, replacing the 2019 authorization. Stock +9% AH.

L2.text: Collectibles segment (peer FNKO read-through) drove growth, not consoles — supplier impact for the new console cycle remains weak. Meme-trade peers AMC, BBBYQ get sympathy bid on the buyback headline; Bitcoin treasury thesis (since GME holds digital assets) brings IBIT/MSTR halo back into focus. Larger retailers (BBY, TGT) lose narrative as GME proves the niche specialty model can flex margins via SG&A discipline.

L3.text: $9.7B cash horde at a $14B market cap turns GME into a quasi-closed-end fund with optionality — at current treasury yields ($9.7B × 4.5% = ~$430M of annual interest income alone), the business has become a yield-plus-collectibles entity rather than a video-game retailer. Macro: with the consumer wallet under pressure from $95 oil and looming hike risk, defensive specialty retail with strong cash conversion stands out.

beneficiaries: FNKO — collectibles read-through to specialty retail
Specialty retail watchlist — proof SG&A discipline can compound
victims: BBY — niche specialty model gaining narrative share
Short interest (GME ~20% of float) — squeeze pressure into open

timeline.D: Open gap +8-12%; squeeze risk on heavy short interest.
timeline.W: Options vol spikes; $2B buyback program ramp gets disclosed weekly.
timeline.M: Capital allocation pivot to active buybacks vs ATM offerings is the structural watch.
timeline.L: Tests whether the cash-flush meme-stock model can compound vs degrade.

news.headline: Yen pierces 160 vs dollar — MoF intervention watch hits the highest alert in 2 years

L1.text: USD/JPY traded right up to 160.00 overnight before pausing on intervention-risk caution — the level breached for the first time since BoJ/MoF jawboning in 2024. The move is driven by the Gulf-shock dollar bid combined with US-Japan yield spread widening as hike odds in the US re-rate up while BoJ Ueda remains cautious. Japan's MoF Kanda-san has signaled 'we will respond to excessive moves'.

L2.text: Direct read: Japanese exporters TM (Toyota), SONY, HMC, NTDOY, Nikkei225 ETF EWJ benefit from competitive devaluation but face intervention-snapback risk. Japanese mega-banks MUFG, SMFG, MFG benefit as JGB yields rise on intervention talk. US importers from Japan (auto OEMs F, GM via component costs) face mixed signal — input cheaper but consumer pricing pressure if F/X intervenes. Carry-trade pairs (AUD/JPY, MXN/JPY) at extreme stretch.

L3.text: If MoF intervenes, expect a 2-3 yen snap-back JPY rally that ripples into gold, Treasuries (TLT bid as carry unwinds), and EM FX volatility. Without intervention, USD/JPY toward 162-165 reopens the Q3 2024 'global vol shock' template. This is the single biggest cross-asset volatility transmission channel right now — VIX at 16 is materially under-pricing the tail.

beneficiaries: UUP — dollar bid as safe-haven + carry magnet
VIX — under-priced tail re-rates if intervention hits
victims: FXY — direct yen short exposure
Carry-trade ETFs (DBV) — sharp risk-off potential
EM FX (KRW, IDR, INR) — sympathy weakness

timeline.D: Intervention watch active during Tokyo/London close hours.
timeline.W: If 160 holds, BoJ rate-policy revision discussion accelerates.
timeline.M: Sets the tone for July BoJ + July FOMC interaction.
timeline.L: Tests whether US-Japan rate divergence can persist without policy escalation.

news.headline: Macy's Q1 earnings before the bell — consumer-pulse read into a stretched tape

L1.text: Macy's reports Q1 sales and EPS at 8:00a ET — CEO Tony Spring's third quarter post-Bold-New-Chapter restructure. Shares are down ~2% YTD but have gained 15% since the last print; the bar is comp-sales sequencing in Bloomingdale's/Bluemercury and any margin commentary on tariff-driven inventory pass-through. With WTI at $95 and gas-pump prices rising again, low/mid-end consumer wallet visibility is the macro question.

L2.text: Read-through to department-store peers KSS, JWN, NORD — all losing share to off-price (TJX, ROST, BURL) and dollar channel (DG, DLTR). Suppliers PVH, RL, TPR, LULU rely on department-store sell-in disclosure for FY guides. Mall REITs SPG, MAC have direct AUO exposure to Macy's anchor-store strategy.

L3.text: Consumer-discretionary divergence theme — premium/affluent (RH, Williams-Sonoma) holding up vs middle-income (M, KSS) compressing — is the structural read of every retail print this quarter. Macro lens: with hike odds rising and oil shock layering on, consumer-credit delinquencies (already at cycle highs at DFS/COF/SYF) are the next leg of the consumer-pulse story.

beneficiaries: DG/DLTR — trade-down beneficiary on oil-shock consumer hit
M (if beat) — relief rally on stretched short positioning
victims: KSS/JWN — sympathy weakness on any Macy's guide-down
PVH/RL — apparel sell-in pressure
SPG/MAC — mall anchor narrative pressure

timeline.D: Pre-market move sets retail-sector tape; XRT direction.
timeline.W: Read into Lululemon, Five Below earnings later this week.
timeline.M: Q2 comp guide is the structural FY26 reset moment.
timeline.L: Bold New Chapter execution timeline tested vs off-price share-take.

news.headline: AI bull-run lifts Asia: Taiwan & Japan equity indexes hit fresh record highs

L1.text: Taiwan TAIEX and Japan Topix both hit fresh record highs overnight as the AI bid stayed dominant despite Gulf-shock crude jumping 2% and S&P 500 futures down 0.3%. TSM (Taipei) and Tokyo Electron led; STM (STMicro) ADR jumped +15.2% in late US trade on a separate AI-content win disclosed at investor day. South Korea closed for elections.

L2.text: Wafer-foundry and lithography supply chain bid: TSM (foundry leader), ASML (EUV monopoly), Tokyo Electron (etch/cleaning), Lam, AMAT. Direct customers NVDA, AVGO, MRVL, AMD ride the supply-chain readiness narrative. Japanese mega-tech (SONY, NEC, Hitachi) levered to AI infrastructure; Korean memory (SK Hynix, Samsung) blocked from today's print but next bid candidates.

L3.text: Decoupling of Asian semi tape from US war-risk overlay shows AI investment thesis has reached escape-velocity status across global allocators — Tokyo and Taipei now setting price for global semi marginal demand. Macro: with yen at 160 and Taiwan dollar managed, currency translation is a tailwind for ADR-listed names.

victims: EWY — South Korea closed, lags on reopen catch-up risk
FXI — China underperforms as APAC rotation favors AI-leaders

timeline.D: ADR opens follow Tokyo/Taipei strength; SMH at the open.
timeline.W: TSM monthly revenue print Thu next wk; AVGO tonight is the read.
timeline.M: September quarter book-to-bill at ASML defines 2027 capex trajectory.
timeline.L: Structural shift of AI demand-setting from US to Asian fabs entrenched.

news.headline: Bitcoin breaks $70K — drops -5.5% in 24h to two-month low as Gulf risk hits crypto

L1.text: Bitcoin slid -5.5% in 24 hours to ~$69,400, a two-month low; Ether -5.25%, BNB -5%. The unwind is driven by hike-odds repricing (CME FedWatch ~42% Dec hike) plus risk-off USD bid from Gulf hostilities. ETF outflows from IBIT and FBTC accelerated Tuesday, and leveraged-perpetual liquidations on the SEC-approved CME-cleared perpetual futures complex amplified the move.

L2.text: Direct pressure on miner equities (MARA, RIOT, CLSK, HUT, IREN) facing oil-driven power cost surge AND BTC weakness — worst combination. Exchanges (COIN, HOOD) face volume-mix pressure as risk-off compresses retail flow. Treasury-bitcoin balance-sheet names (MSTR, MTPLF, Tesla via Musk-X-SpaceX linkages disclosed in S-1) carry mark-to-market vol. Stablecoin issuers (CRCL via Circle) face indirect AUM pressure.

L3.text: Reuters noted that the SEC-approved CME perpetual futures launch is itself unnerving — perpetuals re-introduce 24/7 leverage that historically amplified down-moves. Macro: BTC correlation with NDX is back at 0.7+ on the 30-day rolling; the risk-asset crowding trade unwinds together. Long-duration crypto bulls (Saylor) get tested through hike-cycle persistence.

beneficiaries: TLT — duration bid if hike-narrative cools
victims: MSTR — most levered BTC-treasury proxy
COIN — exchange volume mix shift
MARA/RIOT/CLSK — miner cash-flow squeeze
IBIT/FBTC — direct spot exposure

timeline.D: Watch $68K support; liquidation cascade risk on break.
timeline.W: ETF flow data Mon-Wed shows whether outflow accelerates.
timeline.M: BTC needs ADP miss + soft CPI to reclaim cycle highs.
timeline.L: Tests the post-halving cycle thesis through real risk-off period.

news.headline: US exchanges extend selloff as SEC approves perpetual futures — NDAQ, ICE under pressure

L1.text: Listed-exchange equities extended their selloff Tuesday after the SEC approved perpetual futures contracts — a product structure that allows 24/7 leveraged trading and historically captures meaningful volume share from spot/futures venues. NDAQ and ICE underperformed; CME caught a relative bid as the most likely incumbent venue. Coincidentally, CME also reported record May volume of 33.2M contracts (+15% YoY).

L2.text: Direct beneficiaries: CME (likely listing venue for cleared perpetuals), retail brokers HOOD/SCHW/IBKR (new product to monetize), market makers CBOE/Citadel/Virtu (VIRT) on volume bid. Crypto exchanges (COIN, KRKN private) face partial displacement — perpetuals have been their highest-revenue product offshore. Clearing houses (DTCC, OCC) gain volume.

L3.text: Regulatory liberalization narrative continues under Atkins SEC — perpetuals, spot ETHE 19b-4, looser short-sale reporting all part of an Atkins-led pro-volume framework. Macro lens: more leverage in retail tape = higher VIX-realized historically, supporting vol-of-vol products (VXX). Could become a meaningful flow rotation through Q3.

beneficiaries: CME — venue concentration on cleared perpetuals
victims: NDAQ — listing revenue compression
ICE — futures volume share loss
COIN — partial displacement of perpetual revenue line

timeline.D: NDAQ/ICE relative weakness; CME catches bid.
timeline.W: Atkins SEC commentary on rollout timeline.
timeline.M: First volume-share data from new perpetual venues.
timeline.L: Tests whether retail leverage cycle re-prices broader VIX regime.

news.headline: Apple closes at record — 11th straight up-week, longest streak since 2004

L1.text: Apple closed at a fresh record Tuesday, on pace for an 11th consecutive up-week — the longest streak since 2004. Shares are now +16% YTD, second-best in the Magnificent Seven behind NVDA, after a sluggish start. WWDC (Jun 9) is the next catalyst with on-device Apple Intelligence Gemini integration disclosure widely expected. Recent Needham downgrade to Hold has been completely ignored by the tape.

L2.text: Direct supply-chain beneficiaries: TSM (A-series foundry), SWKS, QRVO (RF), AVGO (RF + custom silicon), QCOM (modem during transition), STX/WDC (memory). Service-bundle peers SPOT, NFLX, GOOGL all read mildly negative if Apple Intelligence + Search deal reshapes traffic. Suppliers Foxconn (HNHPF), Luxshare get pulled.

L3.text: Apple has quietly become the 2026 'AI is real but defensible' trade — buybacks ($110B+ FY24 authorization remaining) + WWDC AI bundle vs valuation re-rate to ~30x FY26 EPS. Macro lens: even with hike risk rising, AAPL's 'consumer subscription + buyback' model is defensively valued vs Nasdaq long-duration names. Mega-cap rotation favoring AAPL/META over MSFT continues.

victims: MSFT — relative mag-7 underperformer (down 8% YTD)
META — relative laggard YTD
Needham (sell-side credibility — downgrade ignored)

timeline.D: Streak watch; profit-taking risk if breaks 11-week run.
timeline.W: WWDC keynote Mon Jun 9 is the event.
timeline.M: iPhone 17 Pro launch cycle commentary at WWDC.
timeline.L: Tests whether on-device AI moat justifies P/E re-rate vs MSFT.

news.headline: CME Group reports record May volume — 33.2M contracts/day, +15% YoY

L1.text: CME Group reported record May volume of 33.2M contracts/day (+15% YoY), driven by interest-rate complex (record SOFR/Fed Funds futures volume on hike-odds repricing) and energy futures (Gulf-shock crude/Brent). Open interest hit records across rates and energy. Adds upside earnings revision pressure into July print.

L2.text: Direct beneficiary CME; market-maker peers VIRT, FLOW, Citadel benefit from volume. Competing futures venues ICE (energy), NDAQ (rates) lose share. Buy-side users (hedge funds, asset managers) face higher rebate-pass-through cost.

L3.text: Volume surge is a structural validation that the macro-volatility regime has shifted — when oil + rates + currency are all moving, futures volumes step up structurally. With Gulf war risk overlaid on the Warsh-Fed transition + hike-odds repricing, this regime is likely persistent through Q3, supporting CME's earnings revision cycle.

victims: ICE — energy futures share competition
NDAQ — rate-futures share competition

timeline.D: CME relative outperformance vs exchange peers.
timeline.W: Sell-side EPS revision cycle next two weeks.
timeline.M: June monthly volume print likely beats again.
timeline.L: Tests whether vol regime shift becomes the multi-year tailwind.

tickerTable.name: TSMC
tickerTable.driver: Asia AI bid pushes TAIEX to ATH

tickerTable.name: Microsoft
tickerTable.driver: Worst day since Feb; AI coding model unveil

buys: XOM/CVX — long Brent >$95 bid; Gulf risk persists
CEG/VST/TLN — TMI restart + hyperscaler PPA template
AVGO — into earnings with custom-silicon TAM upgrade
LMT/RTX — defense reload as missile intercepts mount
CME — record May volume + structural vol regime
TSM/ASML — Asia AI bid escape velocity
sells: MSTR — most levered to BTC drawdown
NDAQ — perpetual futures listing share loss
watchlist: ADP print 8:15a — strong = kills cut narrative outright
ISM Services 10a — sub-50 would be the first contraction in 3 months
Beige Book 2p — Warsh-Fed signal on Gulf-pass-through
AVGO + CRWD AH — sets cyber + semi tape for Thu
USD/JPY 160 break — MoF intervention cross-asset transmission
Iran response to Qeshm strike — overnight headline risk

winners.name: Energy Complex (XOM, CVX, OXY)
winners.why: Brent +1.1% to $97, WTI +2% to $95.40 on Iran-Kuwait-Bahrain missile salvo + Qeshm strike; third straight up-day for crude.

winners.name: Constellation Energy (CEG)
winners.why: FERC waiver clears Three Mile Island restart to serve Microsoft data centers — pulled forward from a 2031 PJM-congestion delay.

winners.name: GameStop (GME)
winners.why: Record Q1 net income ($389.6M), nearly 2x EPS beat ($0.30 vs $0.16), new $2B buyback through 2029; +9% AH.

winners.name: STMicroelectronics (STM)
winners.why: +15.2% on disclosed AI-content win; Asian AI bid pushes TAIEX and Topix to fresh records.

winners.name: CME Group (CME)
winners.why: Record May volume 33.2M contracts/day +15% YoY on rates + energy futures surge; sole big winner of SEC perpetual approval.

losers.name: Strategy (MSTR)
losers.why: BTC -5.5% to ~$69,400 (2-month low) on Gulf risk-off + hike-odds repricing; most levered crypto-treasury proxy in the tape.

losers.name: Airlines (DAL, UAL, LUV)
losers.why: Jet-fuel cost shock from WTI $95 hits Q2/Q3 unit costs; LUV's traditionally un-hedged book is the worst exposed.

losers.name: Rocket Lab (RKLB)
losers.why: SpaceX $75B IPO at $1.75T sets a public comp that immediately compresses RKLB's small-cap launch multiple.

losers.name: Nasdaq (NDAQ)
losers.why: Perpetual futures SEC approval threatens listing/volume share; underperformed listed-exchange peers Tuesday into the decision.

losers.name: Alphabet (GOOGL)
losers.why: $80B equity raise (including $30B public offering + $40B ATM program) creates near-term dilution overhang despite the Berkshire endorsement.

otherStories.headline: Tenaya Therapeutics (TNYA) — MyPEAK-1 Phase 1b/2 interim data on TN-201 for MYBPC3 HCM
otherStories.keyPoint: Near-term gene-therapy biomarker readout drives single-stock vol in cardiomyopathy.
otherStories.beneficiaries: TNYA, peer BMY (mavacamten franchise read-through), CRSP/NTLA on gene-therapy delivery validation

otherStories.headline: Quobly (private) raises $133M Series B for quantum-computing platform
otherStories.keyPoint: Confirms quantum funding bid even as crypto/risk asset wobble; pulls QBTS/RGTI/IONQ momentum.

otherStories.headline: Australia Q1 GDP +0.3% — softest growth since 2023 lockdown period
otherStories.keyPoint: Reinforces RBA dovish framing despite global hike risk; AUD pressure.

otherStories.headline: Applied Aerospace & Defense raises $650M in US IPO (Bloomberg)
otherStories.keyPoint: Defense IPO pricing window opens alongside SpaceX — sympathy bid for defense privates.

otherStories.headline: Microsoft kicks off Build, unveils first AI coding model — MSFT -4.17% on the day
otherStories.keyPoint: Worst MSFT day since February despite the launch; positioning + GOOGL$80B overhang weighs.
otherStories.beneficiaries: GTLB, MDB (coding agents); negative: GOOGL Gemini Code Assist competitive pressure

otherStories.headline: UK Bank Note redesign — foxes, sharks, puffins replace historic figures
otherStories.keyPoint: Symbolic but watched as a confidence/political signal for GBP; sterling sideways.

otherStories.headline: Bayer (BAYRY) — no plans to restructure despite litigation threat
otherStories.keyPoint: Removes near-term spin/breakup catalyst; relief rally limited.
